system.docHtml: Riding governs the ability to control a mounted animal—typically a horse, but potentially a mule, camel, or more exotic beast—in a variety of situations from casual travel to the chaos of mounted combat. The skill encompasses not only balance and posture in the saddle but the subtle communication between rider and mount through leg, hand, and voice commands. An empathic connection with the animal underlies all effective riding, and the most accomplished riders seem to act as a single entity with their mount.
